text: Alveolar collapse (KE 1673) (KE upstream) results from high surface tension in the alveoli, particularly at the end of expiration (when the surface area of the alveoli are at their smallest). Alveolar collapse is difficult to measure on the individual level as collapsed alveoli may open with in breath, but then collapse again. The opening and closing of the alveoli puts stress on the alveolar-capillary membrane that is enhanced in case of additional high surface tension, resulting in loss of integrity between the blood and the air in the lungs (KE 1498) KEdownstream).
